events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`. The "milkshake" oil cap is a classic sign of coolant intrusion into engine oil (emulsified/mayonnaise-like residue), most commonly from a head gasket breach, cracked head/block, or failed oil cooler. This is flagged `fail` / `critical` risk since it indicates an internal engine defect that compromises lubrication — the asset must not be returned to service until the root cause is diagnosed (compression/leak-down test, coolant pressure test, oil cooler inspection) and repaired, followed by a full fluid flush and re-verification.
